subject: Product Management
title: Writing the Product Spec
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Writing the Product Spec.

hook.text: The product spec is the PM's most important artifact — a document that turns vision into a buildable plan.

buildup.text: A good spec answers: what are we building, for whom, why now, and how will we know it worked?

discovery.text: Amazon uses 6-page narratives. Google uses one-pagers. Spotify uses bets. The format varies; the clarity doesn't.

twist.text: Most specs fail not because they're wrong, but because they're vague. Ambiguity is the PM's worst enemy.

climax.text: The spec isn't just a document — it's a thinking tool. Writing it forces you to confront what you don't know.

punchline.text: If you can't write it clearly, you don't understand it yet.

quiz.question: What is the main purpose of a product spec?
options: Turning product vision into a clear, buildable plan the team can align on
